title: xhs-cookies import falls back to wrong container path
body: When the xiaohongshu-mcp container doesn't expose COOKIES_PATH, `agent-reach configure xhs-cookies` falls back to `/app/cookies.json`, but the container's documented default is `/app/data/cookies.json`. This means the cookie write silently lands somewhere the running container never reads from.

title: doctor --json output loses tier/active_backend context useful for scripts
body: `agent-reach doctor --json` just dumps check_all() results, which is great, but there's no documented schema so scripts consuming it have to guess at fields like `tier` and `active_backend`. Could we get a stable documented JSON schema for the doctor report?

title: config.yaml world-readable warning never triggers on Windows
body: format_report() only checks config.yaml permissions `if sys.platform != "win32"`. On Windows, the file can still end up readable by other local accounts (e.g. via inherited ACLs), but doctor gives no warning at all in that case. Should at least attempt an ACL check or note the limitation in the report.

title: _detect_environment() misclassifies headless desktop Linux as a server
body: On a Linux desktop without DISPLAY/WAYLAND_DISPLAY set (e.g. a minimal window manager or SSH'd-into-my-own-machine session), `_detect_environment()` scores this as +1 headless and can tip over into 'server', which then skips OpenCLI-only channels like facebook/instagram even though Chrome is actually running there.

title: Unclear why --channels=all silently drops some channels on servers
body: Ran `agent-reach install --channels=all` on a VPS and facebook/instagram/opencli just vanished from the install log with a one-line Chinese-only note. Could the docs (or an English fallback message) explain up front that OpenCLI-backed channels require a desktop browser session and are always skipped in server mode?
